title: LLM Foundations
description: Core mechanics of large language models, including tokenization, architectures, decoding, and non-determinism.

title: Prompt Engineering
description: Techniques for steering model output, including Chain of Thought, few-shot prompting, and system instructions.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
description: Connecting LLMs to external data sources via chunking, document parsing, and semantic search queries.

title: Vector DBs & Embeddings
description: High-dimensional vector representations of text/media and the database architectures designed to index and query them.

title: Agent Engineering
description: Autonomous loops where language models plan, select and execute tools, evaluate results, and handle stateful actions.

title: MCP & Tooling
description: The Model Context Protocol and the tool definitions, environments, and sandboxes that let LLMs interact with systems.

title: Fine-Tuning & Alignment
description: Adapting models to specific tasks or domains using supervised fine-tuning, LoRA, and preference optimization (RLHF, DPO).

title: LLMOps & Production
description: Serving, deploying, and monitoring LLMs in production, including caching, latency optimization, and cost management.

title: Ethics & Safety
description: Understanding model alignment, bias mitigation, guardrails, and handling potential adversarial inputs.
